basicDetails.schemeName: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for Assistance to Mega Industries: Reimbursement of SGST Paid on Capital Goods

schemeContent.benefits_md: - **SGST Reimbursement:** Full reimbursement of SGST paid on capital goods for eligible units.  - **Long-Term Support:** Reimbursement provided in 20 equal annual installments after a year from commercial production.
schemeContent.exclusions_md: - Goods not considered capital goods under the Gujarat GST Act will not be considered under this scheme.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for Assistance to Mega Industries" aims to strengthen the overall industrial ecosystem in the State and also enable a larger set of manufacturing companies to emulate the adoption of clean energy and make them future-ready to compete in global markets.

children.text: The "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for Assistance to Mega Industries" is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat. It aims to empower the state's dynamic mega-industry sector, which significantly contributes to employment, GDP, and exports. Effective from October 5, 2022, to October 4, 2032, this initiative supports growth, investments, and the vision of "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat" and "Aatmanirbhar Bharat."

The component "Reimbursement of SGST Paid on Capital Goods" offers financial assistance to eligible Mega Industrial Units, providing a 100% reimbursement of SGST paid on capital goods during their investment period. The reimbursement is subject to conditions defined under the Gujarat GST Act 2017.
SGST Reimbursement:
Full reimbursement of SGST paid on capital goods for eligible units.
Long-Term Support:
Reimbursement provided in 20 equal annual installments after a year from commercial production.
Goods not considered capital goods under the Gujarat GST Act will not be considered under this scheme.
Registration Process:
Step-1:
Visit the
Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP)
portal. Click on "New Investor Registration" to access the registration form.
Step-2:
Fill in the Registration Form. Click on "Submit".
Step-3:
Verify the E-mail ID using the link sent on your registered E-mail ID.
Step-4:
After verification, you will be registered successfully.
Post-Registration Process:
Step-1:
Visit the
Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP)
portal. Click on "Login".
Step-2:
Enter the email address and password you used during registration.
Step-3:
Click the "Login" button to access your account.
Step-4:
Now fill in the required details and mandatory documents about your business to complete the application process.
Helpdesk:
Contact Us.

applicationProcess.process_md:
> **Registration Process:** **Step-1:** Visit the [Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP)](https://ifp.gujarat.gov.in/DIGIGOV/) portal. Click on "New Investor Registration" to access the registration form. **Step-2:** Fill in the Registration Form. Click on "Submit". **Step-3:** Verify the E-mail ID using the link sent on your registered E-mail ID. **Step-4:** After verification, you will be registered successfully.
> **Post-Registration Process:** **Step-1:** Visit the [Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP)](https://ifp.gujarat.gov.in/DIGIGOV/) portal. Click on "Login". **Step-2:** Enter the email address and password you used during registration. **Step-3:** Click the "Login" button to access your account. **Step-4:** Now fill in the required details and mandatory documents about your business to complete the application process.
> Helpdesk: [Contact Us.](https://ifp.gujarat.gov.in/DIGIGOV/IFP-pages/contact.jsp?pagedisp=static)

schemeDefinitions.name: Mega Industrial Unit

children.text: A mega industrial unit means an industrial undertaking in thrust sectors having investment in Plant and machinery of at least INR 2,500
crore, AND which provides direct employment to at least 2500 persons. The Mega industrial unit must have obtained acknowledgement/ registration, as the case may be from DPIIT.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md:
A mega industrial unit means an industrial undertaking in thrust sectors having investment in Plant and machinery of at least INR 2,500
crore, AND which provides direct employment to at least 2500 persons. The Mega industrial unit must have obtained acknowledgement/ registration, as the case may be from DPIIT.

schemeDefinitions.name: Date Of Commercial Production (DaCP)

children.text: Gross Fixed Capital Investment means the investment made in land, building, plant and machinery, utilities, tools and equipment, and other fixed assets required to manufacture the end product.
Assets acquired and paid for during the eligible investment period of the project under this scheme shall be considered for determining the gross fixed capital investment.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md:
- Gross Fixed Capital Investment means the investment made in land, building, plant and machinery, utilities, tools and equipment, and other fixed assets required to manufacture the end product.
  - Assets acquired and paid for during the eligible investment period of the project under this scheme shall be considered for determining the gross fixed capital investment.

schemeDefinitions.name: Gross Fixed Capital Investment (GFCI)

children.text: Eligible Fixed Capital Investment means following investment made on or after the date of issue of this GR and upto the last date of eligible investment period as per para 3 of this G.R. Provided that, mega industrial units opting /applying under this scheme as per clause 17.2 or clause 17.3 of this scheme, the investment made during the operative period of the previous scheme till the date of issue of this GR shall also be considered for calculating eligible FCI.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: Eligible Fixed Capital Investment means following investment made on or after the date of issue of this GR and upto the last date of eligible investment period as per para 3 of this G.R. Provided that, mega industrial units opting /applying under this scheme as per clause 17.2 or clause 17.3 of this scheme, the investment made during the operative period of the previous scheme till the date of issue of this GR shall also be considered for calculating eligible FCI.

schemeDefinitions.name: Eligible Fixed Capital Investment (EFCI)

children.text: In case of new industrial unit, the DoCP shall be the date of first sale bill of the product(s) for which the project has been set up. In case of expansion and/or diversification projects, the DoCP shall be the date of first sale bill of the product(s) being manufactured by the
expansion and/or diversification project.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md:
In case of new industrial unit, the DoCP shall be the date of first sale bill of the product(s) for which the project has been set up. In case of expansion and/or diversification projects, the DoCP shall be the date of first sale bill of the product(s) being manufactured by the
expansion and/or diversification project.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. The mega industry must operate as a manufacturing unit.
   1. The unit must have a minimum investment of ₹2,500/- crore in plant and machinery.
   1. The unit must provide direct employment to at least 2,500 persons.
   1. The unit must belong to one of the thrust sectors as defined in the [Guidelines](https://cmogujarat.gov.in/sites/default/files/2023-01/AatmaNirbhar-Gujarat_Industrial-Policy_0.pdf) (Refer Page No. 9).   1. The capital goods must be acquired within the investment period specified under the scheme.
   1. The unit must have commenced commercial production within the timeline set by the scheme.

children.text: The mega industry must operate as a manufacturing unit.
The unit must have a minimum investment of ₹2,500/- crore in plant and machinery.
The unit must provide direct employment to at least 2,500 persons.
The unit must belong to one of the thrust sectors as defined in the
Guidelines
(Refer Page No. 9).
The capital goods must be acquired within the investment period specified under the scheme.
The unit must have commenced commercial production within the timeline set by the scheme.
